Here's the analysis and symbolication workflow for your tombstone:

---

### Crash Summary

The tombstone is from a **.NET Android app using CoreCLR** (JIT runtime, not Mono). The crash is a **SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference)** at fault address `0x48`.

### .NET Runtime Frames Identified

Frames **#01–#06** are from **`libcoreclr.so`** with BuildId:
```
4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
```

Frame #07 is JIT-compiled managed code (anonymous mapping), and #00 is `libc.so` (`abort`).

### Step 1 — Download Debug Symbols

Download the CoreCLR debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server using the `elf-buildid-sym` path:

```bash
curl -sSL -o libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-sym-4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801/libcoreclr.so"
```

### Step 2 — Symbolicate with `llvm-symbolizer`

Run `llvm-symbolizer` against each PC offset from the CoreCLR frames:

```bash
llvm-symbolizer --obj=libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  0x3a81f0 \
  0x3a9244 \
  0x298b10 \
  0x298c48 \
  0x18e3a4 \
  0x18df6c
```

This will output function names and source file locations for each frame, producing a symbolicated backtrace like:

```
#00 pc 0x6ae1c   libc.so            (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x3a81f0  libcoreclr.so      → <function_name> at <source>:<line>
#02 pc 0x3a9244  libcoreclr.so      → <function_name> at <source>:<line>
#03 pc 0x298b10  libcoreclr.so      → <function_name> at <source>:<line>
#04 pc 0x298c48  libcoreclr.so      → <function_name> at <source>:<line>
#05 pc 0x18e3a4  libcoreclr.so      → <function_name> at <source>:<line>
#06 pc 0x18df6c  libcoreclr.so      → <function_name> at <source>:<line>
#07 pc 0x307c    <anonymous>        (JIT-compiled managed code — needs managed debugger)
```

> **Note:** The symbol server returned 404 for this BuildId, which means the debug symbols may not be publicly published for this particular CoreCLR build. If the download fails, you can obtain the unstripped `libcoreclr.so` from your app's build output (e.g., the `Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.android-arm64` NuGet package matching your .NET SDK version).
